subject: Chemistry
title: Bleach: The Oxidizing Cleaner
description: A micro-lesson that makes Bleach: The Oxidizing Cleaner usable.

hook.text: Bleach doesn't just clean—it destroys color molecules by ripping electrons from their bonds.

buildup.text: Household bleach is sodium hypochlorite dissolved in water. It's a powerful oxidizing agent.

discovery.text: The hypochlorite ion (OCl⁻) steals electrons from colored organic molecules, breaking the bonds that absorb light.

twist.text: Never mix bleach with ammonia. It produces chloramine gas—toxic enough to send people to the hospital.

climax.text: Bleach kills bacteria by oxidizing their cell membranes. Same chemistry, different target.

punchline.text: Steals electrons, breaks color, kills germs. Bleach is brute-force chemistry.

quiz.question: Why should you never mix bleach with ammonia?
